name: 格奥尔格·弗里德里希·亨德尔
brief: 亨德尔出生于德国哈雷，与巴赫同年出生却走上了截然不同的道路。他游历意大利学习歌剧，后定居英国伦敦，成为英国音乐生活的核心人物。亨德尔是一位精明的商人与剧院经理，他的音乐具有强烈的公共性与戏剧性。晚年双目失明后仍坚持演出与创作。他被安葬在威斯敏斯特教堂，是英国最受尊崇的作曲家之一。
briefEn: Handel was born in Halle, Germany, the same year as Bach, yet followed a vastly different path. He traveled to Italy to study opera, then settled in London, where he became a central figure in British musical life. A shrewd businessman and theater impresario, Handel wrote music of powerful public grandeur and dramatic force. Even after going blind in his later years, he continued to perform and compose. He was buried in Westminster Abbey and remains one of the most revered composers in British history.
contribution: 亨德尔将意大利歌剧传统移植到英国，并在歌剧衰落后开创了英语清唱剧（Oratorio）这一全新体裁。《弥赛亚》成为西方音乐史上最常被演出的声乐作品之一。他的协奏大协奏曲（Concerti Grossi）为管弦乐发展提供了重要范本，其宏大的合唱写作技巧影响了海顿、莫扎特乃至贝多芬。
contributionEn: Handel transplanted the Italian operatic tradition to England and, when opera fell out of fashion, pioneered the English-language oratorio as an entirely new genre. Messiah became one of the most frequently performed vocal works in Western music history. His Concerti Grossi provided vital models for orchestral development, and his magnificent choral writing influenced Haydn, Mozart, and Beethoven alike.
style: 亨德尔的音乐以宏伟壮丽、旋律优美著称，具有强烈的戏剧张力和叙事能力。与巴赫内省的对位风格不同，亨德尔追求的是外向的、具有公共仪式感的音乐效果。他的合唱写作气势磅礴，独唱旋律则兼具意大利式的优美与英国式的庄严。他善于用简洁有力的音乐语言直击人心。
styleEn: Handel's music is celebrated for its grandeur, melodic beauty, and powerful dramatic narrative. Unlike Bach's introspective contrapuntal style, Handel pursued an outward, ceremonially public musical effect. His choral writing is magnificently imposing, while his solo vocal lines combine Italian lyricism with English solemnity. He excelled at striking straight to the heart with concise, forceful musical language.

famousScores.name: 《弥赛亚》 HWV 56
famousScores.desc: 清唱剧巨作，其中《哈利路亚合唱》家喻户晓
famousScores.descEn: A towering oratorio whose Hallelujah chorus is known the world over
famousScores.detail:
《弥赛亚》创作于1741年，仅用24天便告完成——这一传说已成为西方音乐史上最著名的佳话之一。亨德尔在都柏林好友詹宁斯为其提供的圣经文本基础上，以惊人的速度完成了这部分为三个部分、共五十余个乐章的宏篇巨制。1741年12月首演于都柏林，大获成功；1743年伦敦首演时，国王乔治二世在《哈利路亚》合唱响起时起立致敬，此后这一传统延续至今。

关于亨德尔创作此曲时的精神状态，流传着一段令人动容的记述：他在写下《哈利路亚合唱》后泪流满面，对仆人说：「我以为上帝与我同在。」无论此事真假，这首合唱的确拥有一种超越性的精神力量。《哈利路亚》的结尾以最高音区的「永远为王」在全奏和弦上反复冲击，制造出一种宗教仪式般的震撼，令数百年来无数听众热泪盈眶。

《弥赛亚》是迄今为止被演出次数最多的西方声乐作品之一，每年圣诞节和复活节，全球无数合唱团都会演唱这部作品。海顿在1791年伦敦听过此曲后深受震撼，直接促成了《创世记》的诞生。莫扎特受委托为其重新配器（K. 572版本）。这部作品将英语语言提升为与意大利语并列的高雅音乐语言，深刻影响了英国声乐传统，成为英国合唱文化的核心。
famousScores.detailEn:
Messiah was composed in 1741, completed in an astonishing 24 days — one of the most celebrated feats of creative speed in music history. Working from a scriptural text compiled by his friend Charles Jennens, Handel produced this three-part oratorio of over fifty movements with almost supernatural swiftness. The Dublin premiere in December 1741 was a triumph; at the London premiere in 1743, King George II reportedly rose to his feet during the Hallelujah chorus, establishing a tradition that continues worldwide to this day.

Handel is said to have wept while writing the Hallelujah chorus, telling his servant: 'I did think I did see all Heaven before me, and the great God Himself.' Whether or not the story is literal, the music carries an unmistakable quality of transport — the final blazing 'King of Kings' hammered home over full orchestra and chorus has brought tears to audiences for nearly three centuries.

Messiah is one of the most frequently performed large-scale vocal works in Western history, performed by choirs worldwide every Christmas and Easter. Haydn heard it in London in 1791 and was so shaken that he was inspired to compose The Creation. Mozart re-orchestrated it (K. 572) at Baron van Swieten's request. The work elevated the English language as a vehicle for great music alongside Italian, and remains the cornerstone of the British choral tradition.

famousScores.name: 《水上音乐》 HWV 348–350
famousScores.desc: 为英王乔治一世泰晤士河游船而作的管弦乐组曲
famousScores.descEn: An orchestral suite composed for King George I's royal barge procession on the Thames
famousScores.detail:
《水上音乐》由三套组曲构成（F大调、G大调、D大调），创作于1717年。背景是英王乔治一世在泰晤士河上举行的盛大游船宴会。据当时的记载，国王的御舟旁边跟着一条乐船，上载50名乐手，亨德尔亲自指挥，乐声飘荡在河面上，引得两岸观众和无数小舟云集。整个演出持续了数小时，据说国王听得兴高采烈，要求乐队连奏了三遍。

这段历史有一个有趣的政治背景：乔治一世来自德国汉诺威，他与亨德尔早年在汉诺威就认识，亨德尔曾受雇于汉诺威宫廷。但1710年亨德尔未经允许便私自前往伦敦，后来又不辞而别，导致国王颇为不满。《水上音乐》的演出据说是亨德尔借助好友的安排「重新赢得国王欢心」的机会——一场精心设计的音乐和解。

《水上音乐》的D大调组曲中那首著名的「阿拉」（Alla Hornpipe）是音乐史上最迷人的行进曲风旋律之一，至今在英国各类正式场合仍频繁使用。整套组曲对法国组曲和意大利协奏曲风格的融合展现了亨德尔综合多国传统的天赋，其明朗欢快的气质成为「亨德尔风格」最鲜明的代表之一。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Water Music consists of three suites (in F major, G major, and D major) written for King George I's grand Thames river party in 1717. Contemporary accounts describe a barge carrying fifty musicians alongside the royal vessel, with Handel himself conducting as the music drifted across the water — drawing crowds to both riverbanks. The entertainment lasted several hours, and the king reportedly enjoyed it so much he demanded the orchestra repeat the entire programme three times.

There is a delicious political backstory: George I had known Handel from their shared years in Hanover, where Handel had been employed at court. In 1710, Handel had slipped away to London without permission and then overstayed his leave, causing royal displeasure. The Water Music performance was allegedly arranged by a mutual friend as Handel's opportunity to win back the king's favour — an elaborate musical act of reconciliation.

The Hornpipe from the D major Suite is one of the most irresistible march-like tunes in all of Handel, still heard at formal British occasions. The suite as a whole demonstrates Handel's genius for blending French, Italian, and German stylistic traditions into something perfectly suited to grand public occasion — the archetype of what posterity would call 'Handelian' grandeur.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Handel Water Music Trevor Pinnock English Concert

famousScores.name: 《皇家焰火音乐》 HWV 351
famousScores.nameEn: Music for the Royal Fireworks, HWV 351
famousScores.desc: 庆祝亚琛和约的大型户外管弦乐作品
famousScores.descEn: A grand open-air outdoor orchestral work celebrating the Treaty of Aix-la-Chapelle
famousScores.detail:
《皇家焰火音乐》创作于1749年，为庆祝结束奥地利王位继承战争的亚琛和约而作。英王乔治二世命令在伦敦绿园举行盛大的露天庆典，并特别要求亨德尔：不要小提琴，只用军乐编制。亨德尔最初抵制这一要求，最终的版本包含9支双簧管、9支大管、24支法国号、12支小号、3对定音鼓和一批打击乐器，阵容壮观。正式演出前的公开彩排在瓦克斯豪尔花园举行，吸引了超过12000名观众，导致伦敦桥交通堵塞三个小时。

烟火之夜本身却是一场喜剧性的灾难：华美的烟火装置因意外失火，整个搭台付之一炬，引发踩踏混乱。但现场演奏的音乐却完全不受影响，成为当晚唯一的成功。这一对比——音乐的不朽与烟火的瞬息——成为此后人们谈论这部作品时不可回避的隐喻。

《皇家焰火音乐》的开篇「序曲」是亨德尔管弦乐写作的巅峰——法国式的附点节奏在铜管齐鸣中缓缓推进，雄浑壮丽，充满王者气象。亨德尔后来补充了弦乐声部，使这部作品既可以在户外以军乐队风格演奏，也可以在音乐厅以完整管弦乐团呈现。与《水上音乐》并列，它是亨德尔最具代表性的户外庆典音乐，至今在各类庆典场合被频繁演奏。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Music for the Royal Fireworks was composed in 1749 to celebrate the Treaty of Aix-la-Chapelle ending the War of Austrian Succession. King George II commanded a grand outdoor celebration in Green Park, London, and specifically ordered Handel to use no violins — only military-style wind instruments. Handel initially resisted; the final version deploys nine oboes, nine bassoons, twenty-four horns, twelve trumpets, three pairs of kettledrums, and percussion — a spectacular brass and wind army.

The public rehearsal at Vauxhall Gardens drew over 12,000 spectators and caused a three-hour traffic jam on London Bridge. The night of the fireworks itself was farcical: the elaborate wooden fireworks structure caught fire and burned down, causing panic in the crowd. Yet the music played on undisturbed — making it, ironically, the only successful element of the entire evening. This contrast between the music's endurance and the fireworks' destruction became an irresistible metaphor for the work's immortality.

The opening Overture is the summit of Handel's wind writing — French-style dotted rhythms advancing majestically through a blaze of brass, magnificent and regal. Handel later added string parts, making the work playable either in full orchestral dress or as pure military wind band music. Together with the Water Music, it defines the grandest tradition of Handelian public ceremonial and is still a favourite at outdoor celebrations worldwide.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Handel Royal Fireworks John Eliot Gardiner

famousScores.name: 《塞尔斯》中的广板
famousScores.desc: 又名「亨德尔的广板」，最广为人知的优美旋律之一
famousScores.descEn: Known as 'Handel's Largo,' one of the most beloved melodies in all of classical music
famousScores.detail:
「亨德尔的广板」（实为行板，Larghetto）是歌剧《塞尔斯》（Serse，HWV 40，1738年）第一幕的开场咏叹调「赐荫于我」（Ombra mai fu）。波斯王薛西斯（即塞尔斯）爱慕一棵梧桐树的荫凉，对它唱出这首赞美之歌——这个荒诞的戏剧情境（一位国王爱上了一棵树）本是意大利正歌剧传统中的讽刺对象，亨德尔却为其配上了音乐史上最动人的旋律之一，产生了一种奇异的崇高感。

这首曲子的传播史本身就是一个关于音乐流传的有趣故事。歌剧《塞尔斯》在亨德尔生前票房惨败，上演5场便草草收场。然而这首咏叹调却在19世纪被单独提取出来，以「广板」之名广泛传播，完全脱离了其戏剧原境。19世纪的出版商将它标注为「Largo」（其实原谱标记是Larghetto），这个错误的速度标记却反而帮助它以庄严迟缓的方式广为流传，成为葬礼、婚礼、典礼等各类场合的常客。

「亨德尔的广板」被改编为无数版本：小提琴、大提琴、管风琴、弦乐四重奏乃至萨克斯风，几乎可以为所有乐器演奏。这首咏叹调所体现的亨德尔旋律才华——简洁、庄重、令人感动而不感伤——是他有别于同时代作曲家的最显著特征。戏剧讽刺变成了精神慰藉，这种历史误解反而创造了一种永恒。
famousScores.detailEn:
The famous 'Handel's Largo' is in fact a Larghetto — the opening aria 'Ombra mai fu' ('Never was there a shade') from the opera Serse (HWV 40, 1738). The Persian king Xerxes sings this love song to a plane tree whose shade he adores — an absurd dramatic conceit in the Italian opera seria tradition, meant partly in jest — yet Handel set it to one of the most sublimely beautiful melodies he ever wrote, creating an effect of unintended and inexplicable grandeur.

The aria's subsequent life is a fascinating story of musical transformation. The opera Serse was a commercial failure in Handel's lifetime, closing after just five performances. Yet the aria was extracted in the 19th century, published under the misnomer 'Largo' (the original tempo marking is Larghetto), and circulated as an independent piece entirely divorced from its theatrical context. The wrong tempo marking paradoxically helped — played slowly and solemnly, it became a fixture at funerals, weddings, and state occasions.

Arranged for virtually every instrument imaginable — violin, cello, organ, saxophone, strings — 'Handel's Largo' encapsulates the composer's melodic genius at its most distilled: simple, dignified, moving without being sentimental. That a satirical royal love song to a tree became the world's favourite ceremonial music is perhaps the most charming irony in the entire Baroque repertoire.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Handel Largo Ombra mai fu Victoria de los Angeles

famousScores.name: 《所罗门》 HWV 67
famousScores.desc: 清唱剧，其中「示巴女王的到来」是管弦乐名段
famousScores.descEn: An oratorio whose 'Arrival of the Queen of Sheba' is a celebrated orchestral showpiece
famousScores.detail:
《所罗门》清唱剧创作于1748年，是亨德尔晚期最辉煌的作品之一。故事取材于圣经《列王纪上》，描写以色列王所罗门的智慧与荣耀、他的圣殿奉献以及示巴女王的来访。全曲分三个部分，合唱写作极为辉煌——亨德尔使用了双合唱团，在部分段落创造出八声部的壮丽效果，被认为是他合唱写作的最高成就之一。

「示巴女王的到来」（The Arrival of the Queen of Sheba）是第三部分的管弦乐间奏曲，以双簧管和弦乐队在快速音阶和华丽琶音中交相呼应，充满异国情调的优雅与活力。这段音乐仅有几分钟，却成为20世纪最受欢迎的管弦乐片段之一——尤其是1981年查尔斯王子与戴安娜王妃的世纪婚礼上，英国男高音克里斯托弗·罗伯特和大号手前进时，现场演奏了这首曲子，从此与英国皇室仪式深度绑定。

《所罗门》在19世纪被忽视，20世纪下半叶的「亨德尔复兴」使其重新登上舞台。指挥家约翰·埃利奥特·加德纳的录音（1984年）被认为是还原这部作品本来辉煌的里程碑式录音。今天，《所罗门》被视为与《弥赛亚》《以色列人在埃及》并列的亨德尔清唱剧三大杰作，其宏大的合唱织体和戏剧叙事能力充分展示了亨德尔在清唱剧领域登峰造极的技艺。
famousScores.detailEn:
Solomon was composed in 1748 and stands as one of the supreme achievements of Handel's later years. Drawing on the biblical account in 1 Kings, it depicts the wisdom and splendor of King Solomon, the dedication of his Temple, and the famous visit of the Queen of Sheba. The choral writing employs a double choir, creating passages of eight-part magnificence widely considered the pinnacle of Handel's choral art.

The 'Arrival of the Queen of Sheba' — a brief orchestral sinfonia in the third act — became one of the most universally beloved orchestral miniatures of the twentieth century, with its oboes and strings trading sparkling scales and arpeggios in exotic, high-spirited dialogue. Its fame was sealed for a generation by its use at the 1981 wedding of Prince Charles and Diana Spencer, cementing its association with royal British ceremony.

Solomon languished in neglect throughout the 19th century; the Handel revival of the later 20th century restored it to the stage. John Eliot Gardiner's landmark recording of 1984 is widely credited with revealing its full magnificence to modern audiences. Today it stands alongside Messiah and Israel in Egypt as one of the three greatest Handel oratorios, demonstrating the full range of his choral genius — from intimate tenderness to overwhelming public grandeur.

famousScores.name: 《赞美上主》 HWV 232
famousScores.desc: 亨德尔22岁的早期杰作，展现惊人的戏剧与对位天才
famousScores.descEn: A youthful masterpiece by the 22-year-old Handel, revealing extraordinary dramatic and contrapuntal genius
famousScores.detail:
《赞美上主》（Dixit Dominus）HWV 232是亨德尔1707年旅居罗马时创作的拉丁语圣咏作品，以《诗篇》第110篇为文本，用于天主教礼拜中的晚祷仪式。此时的亨德尔年仅22岁，在意大利游学期间已展现出令人叹服的成熟度。全曲包含九个乐章，以五声部独唱、四声部合唱和弦乐队构成，风格博采意大利对位传统与罗马巴洛克风格之长。

最令人震惊的乐章是第五乐章「他要打碎他们」（Dominus a dextris tuis）：两个女高音声部以密接和应的方式相互追逐，合唱以斩钉截铁的和弦呼应，共同描绘神圣审判的雷霆之怒，力度变化剧烈，音乐中充满了一种几近暴力的戏剧张力。现代学者认为，这首作品已预示了亨德尔后来在《弥赛亚》中所达到的合唱高峰。

《赞美上主》在亨德尔生前几乎从未再被演出，直至20世纪末才随着巴洛克音乐的全面复兴而重新引起广泛关注。今天，它与维瓦尔第的《光荣颂》一道，被视为巴洛克晚期最重要的拉丁语礼拜音乐之一。这部作品改变了人们对亨德尔的认识：他不仅是一位擅长公众场合的壮丽风格作曲家，更是一位具有深刻宗教内省力的天才。
famousScores.detailEn:
Dixit Dominus HWV 232 was composed by Handel in 1707 during his Italian sojourn in Rome. Setting Psalm 110 for the Catholic Vespers service, it was written when Handel was just twenty-two — yet reveals a maturity of technique and dramatic imagination that astonished his Italian contemporaries. The nine movements deploy five vocal soloists, four-part chorus, and strings, blending Roman Baroque ceremonial style with Handel's already distinctive dramatic flair.

The most electrifying movement is the fifth, 'Dominus a dextris tuis': two soprano voices chase each other in tight imitation while the chorus punctuates with hammer-blow chords, painting divine judgment with an almost violent dramatic energy. Modern scholars regard this as a clear prefiguration of the choral grandeur Handel would later achieve in Messiah.

Dixit Dominus was virtually unknown for much of music history, rediscovered only as part of the late-20th-century Baroque revival. Today it stands alongside Vivaldi's Gloria as one of the most important sacred Latin works of the late Baroque. The piece has reshaped our understanding of Handel: far from being merely a composer of grand public splendor, he could achieve intense, introspective spiritual force at the age of twenty-two.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Handel Dixit Dominus John Eliot Gardiner

famousScores.name: 《阿尔西娜》 HWV 34
famousScores.desc: 亨德尔最后的伟大意大利歌剧，充满魔幻与情感深度
famousScores.descEn: Handel's last great Italian opera, rich in magic, passion, and psychological depth
famousScores.detail:
《阿尔西娜》（Alcina）是亨德尔1735年的歌剧杰作，取材于意大利文艺复兴史诗《疯狂的奥兰多》（Ariosto）中女巫阿尔西娜的故事。故事讲述女巫阿尔西娜将爱慕她的男人变成野兽、岩石或植物，最终被英雄鲁杰罗的真爱打破魔咒，失去魔力后的她陷入绝望的悲剧。全剧最著名的唱段「放开我吧」（Mi restano le lagrime）和「当那恶人」（Ah, mio cor）展现了亨德尔对人物心理刻画的惊人深度。

《阿尔西娜》在1735年伦敦首演时，由当时最著名的假声男高音法里内利的对手卡斯特拉尼出演，亨德尔亲自弹奏羽管键琴指挥。演出使用了当时伦敦舞台最复杂的机械装置呈现魔法效果，但即便如此，意大利歌剧在伦敦已经急剧失去公众兴趣，《阿尔西娜》只演了18场便停演，标志着亨德尔意大利歌剧生涯的最后辉煌。

此后两个世纪，《阿尔西娜》几乎完全从舞台上消失。20世纪60年代，指挥家理查德·博宁格与女高音琼·萨瑟兰合作录制的版本使这部歌剧重焕光彩；80年代起，随着「本真演奏」运动的兴起，这部歌剧更是成为巴洛克歌剧舞台上最受欢迎的剧目之一。今天，它被视为亨德尔歌剧中与《尤里乌斯·恺撒》《塞尔斯》并列的三大杰作。
famousScores.detailEn:
Alcina (HWV 34, 1735) is Handel's final great Italian opera, drawn from Ariosto's Renaissance epic Orlando Furioso. The sorceress Alcina enchants men who love her, transforming them into animals, rocks, and plants; when the hero Ruggiero's true love breaks her spell, she is left powerless, abandoned, and devastated. Arias such as 'Ah, mio cor' and 'Mi restano le lagrime' achieve a depth of psychological portraiture rarely matched in Baroque opera.

At its London premiere in 1735, Alcina employed the most sophisticated stage machinery available, producing spectacular magical illusions. Handel conducted from the harpsichord. Yet Italian opera had by then largely lost its London audience: the opera ran for only eighteen performances before closing — the last flowering of Handel's operatic career before he turned definitively to oratorio.

For two centuries Alcina was virtually unknown outside of specialized scholarship. In the 1960s, conductor Richard Bonynge and soprano Joan Sutherland's recordings brought it to wide attention; from the 1980s, the period-instrument movement made it one of the most beloved operas in the Baroque repertoire. Today it stands alongside Giulio Cesare and Serse as one of Handel's three greatest operas, celebrated for its unparalleled fusion of magical spectacle and intimate human emotion.

famousScores.name: 《大协奏曲》 Op.6
famousScores.desc: 十二首协奏大协奏曲，巴洛克管弦乐文献中的不朽丰碑
famousScores.descEn: Twelve concerti grossi — an immortal monument of Baroque orchestral literature
famousScores.detail:
《大协奏曲》Op.6共十二首，创作于1739年，是亨德尔仅用一个月时间完成的惊人创作壮举。这套作品延续了科雷利奠定的协奏大协奏曲传统——以小提琴和大提琴组成的「独奏群」（Concertino）与完整弦乐合奏队（Ripieno）在音响上相互对话——亨德尔在这个框架内注入了无限的风格变化：从庄严的法国序曲风格到轻盈的吉格，从深情的慢乐章到活泼的乡村舞曲，无一相同。

这套作品是在亨德尔遭遇重大人生挫败后写成的：他所主导的意大利歌剧事业在伦敦彻底崩溃，他本人也一度破产，并患上了严重的抑郁症。然而正是在这一时期，他转向了器乐写作和清唱剧，创造出了生命中最伟大的作品群。Op.6的十二首协奏曲中有数首展现出一种明显深沉的、冥想性的情绪，被认为是亨德尔内心世界最真实的自白。

《大协奏曲》Op.6与科雷利Op.6和巴赫《勃兰登堡协奏曲》并列，是巴洛克协奏大协奏曲传统的三座高峰。亨德尔在其中展示的配器色彩感和形式灵活性对海顿早期交响曲的写作产生了直接影响。这套作品的出版在当时取得了空前的商业成功，在英国和欧洲大陆广为流传，确立了亨德尔作为器乐作曲家的卓越声誉。
famousScores.detailEn:
The twelve Concerti Grossi Op. 6 were composed in 1739, reputedly written in a single month — another astonishing feat of Handelian productivity. They continue the tradition of the concerto grosso established by Corelli, pitting a small group of soloists (concertino: two violins and cello) against the full string orchestra (ripieno) in musical dialogue. Within this framework, Handel achieves endless variety: from stately French overtures to nimble gigues, from heartfelt slow movements to rustic dances — no two concertos alike.

The circumstances of their composition are poignant: Handel wrote them in the aftermath of the collapse of his Italian opera enterprise in London, a period of near-bankruptcy and serious depression. It was precisely out of this creative crisis that he turned to instrumental music and oratorio, producing the greatest works of his life. Several of the Op. 6 concertos carry an unusually introspective, even brooding character — widely heard as an unusually personal statement from a composer usually known for outward brilliance.

Alongside Corelli's own Op. 6 and Bach's Brandenburgs, these concertos represent the three summits of the Baroque concerto grosso tradition. Their influence on Haydn's early symphonies is direct and well documented. Published in 1740, they achieved unprecedented commercial success across Britain and Europe, cementing Handel's reputation as a master of instrumental music alongside his fame as a vocal composer.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Handel Concerti Grossi Op.6 Trevor Pinnock

famousScores.name: 《尤里乌斯·恺撒》 HWV 17
famousScores.desc: 亨德尔最伟大的意大利歌剧，戏剧性与旋律美兼备
famousScores.descEn: Handel's greatest Italian opera, combining dramatic mastery with supreme melodic beauty
famousScores.detail:
《尤里乌斯·恺撒在埃及》（Giulio Cesare in Egitto）HWV 17是亨德尔1724年的歌剧杰作，被普遍认为是他全部四十余部意大利歌剧中最伟大的一部。剧情以罗马将军恺撒抵达埃及、与埃及女王克利奥帕特拉相遇相爱为主线，穿插宫廷阴谋与政治斗争，充满戏剧张力。克利奥帕特拉的三首咏叹调——尤其是「我哭泣于自己的命运」（Piangero la sorte mia）和「我充满了忧愁」（V'adoro, pupille）——是巴洛克歌剧中最美丽的抒情页面。

首演于伦敦国王剧院时，剧中克利奥帕特拉一角由意大利女高音弗朗切斯卡·库佐尼出演，她以极度挑剔著称，据记载曾拒绝演唱亨德尔为她写的某首咏叹调。盛怒之下，亨德尔将她悬空举起，威胁说如果她再不照谱演唱就从窗户扔下去。此后，库佐尼不得不乖乖就范，而这首「顽固」的咏叹调在首演夜博得了满堂喝彩。这段奇闻成为音乐史上最著名的作曲家-演唱家冲突故事之一。

和大多数亨德尔歌剧一样，《朱利叶斯·恺撒》在19世纪几乎无人演出。1920年代，德国歌剧界率先复兴了这部作品，此后各大歌剧院陆续将其搬上舞台。20世纪70年代以来，指挥家莱因哈特·戈贝尔、威廉·克里斯蒂等人的本真演奏版本将其推向了国际舞台的中心。今天，《尤里乌斯·恺撒》被视为可与莫扎特歌剧并列的西方歌剧史上的不朽杰作。
famousScores.detailEn:
Giulio Cesare in Egitto (HWV 17, 1724) is universally regarded as the greatest of Handel's forty-odd Italian operas. The plot follows Julius Caesar's arrival in Egypt, his encounter and love affair with Cleopatra, and the political intrigues of the Egyptian court. Cleopatra's three great arias — especially 'Piangero la sorte mia' and 'V'adoro, pupille' — are among the most ravishingly beautiful pages in all of Baroque vocal music.

At the King's Theatre premiere, Cleopatra was sung by the notoriously temperamental Italian soprano Francesca Cuzzoni, who reportedly refused to sing one of her arias as written. According to legend, Handel seized her and threatened to throw her out the window if she did not comply — whereupon she sang it perfectly, and it brought the house down on opening night. This confrontation has become one of the most famous composer-singer conflicts in music history.

Like most Handel operas, Giulio Cesare vanished from the stage in the 19th century. German opera houses pioneered its revival in the 1920s, and from the 1970s onward, period-instrument conductors such as René Jacobs and William Christie placed it at the center of the international operatic stage. Today it is regarded as one of the immortal masterpieces of Western opera — a work that stands comfortably alongside Mozart in psychological depth, theatrical craft, and sheer melodic beauty.

famousScores.name: 《扫罗》 HWV 53
famousScores.desc: 亨德尔最具戏剧张力的清唱剧，对人类嫉妒与悲剧的深刻描绘
famousScores.descEn: Handel's most dramatically intense oratorio — a profound study of jealousy, power, and tragedy
famousScores.detail:
《扫罗》清唱剧创作于1738年，1739年在伦敦皇家歌剧院首演，是亨德尔告别意大利歌剧转向英语清唱剧的关键作品。故事取材于圣经《撒母耳记》，描绘以色列王扫罗因嫉妒大卫的功绩而精神崩溃，最终走向悲剧覆灭的过程。亨德尔对扫罗心理状态的音乐刻画——从雄心勃勃到嫉妒发狂，从短暂的悔悟到彻底的堕落——在巴洛克音乐中具有近乎莎士比亚式的深度。

作品中有一个惊人的音效设计：为配合大卫击杀歌利亚的庆典场面，亨德尔特别订制了一架超大型的管风琴式「卡里永」（Carillon）——用木槌敲击一排调音铁管，制造出空灵的钟声效果，这在当时的伦敦舞台上是前所未有的。此外，全曲中的葬礼进行曲（Dead March）后来被广泛用于英国皇室葬礼，成为英国哀悼仪式的象征之一，甚至比亨德尔更著名的许多作品更深地嵌入了英国的文化基因。

20世纪的音乐学家重新认识到《扫罗》是亨德尔清唱剧中心理最复杂、戏剧最成熟的作品，甚至认为它在叙事深度上超越了《弥赛亚》。指挥家约翰·埃利奥特·加德纳、马克·明科夫斯基等人的演录为这部作品赢得了新一代听众的热爱。《扫罗》证明，亨德尔不仅仅是公众庆典的作曲家，更是能够深入人类黑暗灵魂的伟大戏剧家。
famousScores.detailEn:
Saul (HWV 53) was composed in 1738 and premiered at the King's Theatre, London, in January 1739 — a pivotal work marking Handel's decisive turn from Italian opera to English oratorio. Based on the biblical account in 1 Samuel, it traces the psychological disintegration of King Saul, consumed by jealousy of the young David's fame, spiraling toward madness and tragic destruction. Handel's musical portrait of Saul's inner life — from ambition to jealous rage, from brief repentance to total collapse — achieves a near-Shakespearean depth of character.

The score contains one of the most novel sound effects in 18th-century theater: for the triumphal celebrations following David's victory over Goliath, Handel commissioned a specially built 'carillon' — a set of tuned metal tubes struck with hammers — producing an ethereal bell-like sound never previously heard on a London stage. The work's 'Dead March' was adopted for British royal funerals and state occasions, embedding itself more deeply into the nation's ceremonial culture than many of Handel's more famous works.

Modern musicologists regard Saul as Handel's psychologically richest and dramatically most mature oratorio — some consider it a more profound work than Messiah. Landmark recordings by John Eliot Gardiner and Marc Minkowski have won it a devoted new audience. Saul proves definitively that Handel was not merely the composer of grand public occasions, but a consummate dramatic artist capable of penetrating the darkest corners of the human soul.

influence: 亨德尔对英国音乐传统的影响延续至今，每年的逍遥音乐会（Proms）仍以他的作品为重要曲目。海顿在伦敦听到《弥赛亚》后深受震撼，直接催生了《创世记》与《四季》。贝多芬晚年曾说「亨德尔是最伟大的作曲家」。莫扎特曾为《弥赛亚》重新配器。亨德尔将音乐从宫廷带向公众，开创了现代音乐会文化的雏形。
influenceEn: Handel's impact on the British musical tradition endures to this day — his works remain a cornerstone of the annual Proms. Haydn was so profoundly moved upon hearing Messiah in London that it directly inspired The Creation and The Seasons. In his later years Beethoven declared Handel 'the greatest composer.' Mozart re-orchestrated Messiah out of deep admiration. By bringing music from the court to the public, Handel laid the groundwork for modern concert culture.
